ocr.text: The table presents findings from various studies regarding coronary anomalies, detailing the author/year of publication,
prevalence (%), most common coronary anomaly, imaging modality, and country. Yamanaka et al. 1990 [1] reported a
prevalence of 1.30% (1,686 of 126,595) with the most common coronary anomaly being absent LMCA with separate origin of
LAD and LCX, utilizing CAG in the USA. Kaku et al. 1996 [2] found a prevalence of 0.31% (56 of 17,731) for anomalous
origin of RCA from LSV, also employing CAG in Japan. Kardos et al. 1997 [3] documented a prevalence of 1.34% (103 of
7,694) with the same anomaly as Yamanaka et al., using CAG in Central Europe. Garg et al.
Author/year of publication  Prevalence (%)  Most common coronary anomaly  Imaging modality  Country
Yamanaka et al. 1990 [1]  1.30 (1,686 of 126,595)  Absent LMCA with separate origin of LAD and LCX  CAG  USA
Kaku et al. 1996 [2]  0.31 (56 of 17,731)  Anomalous origin of RCA from LSV  CAG  Japan
Kardos et al. 1997 [3]  1.34 (103 of 7,694)  Absent LMCA with separate origin of LAD and LCX  CAG  Central Europe
Garg et al. 2000 [4]  0.95 (39 of 4,100  Anomalous origin of RCA from LSV/NAS  CAG  India
Yildiz et al. 2010 [5]  0.90 (112 of 12,457)  Absent LMCA with separate origin of LAD and LCX  CAG  Turkey
Erol et al. 2011 [6]  1.96 (53 of 2,096)  Absent LMCA with separate origin of LAD and LCX/Origin of RCA from LSV  64MDCT  Turkey
Fujimoto et al. 2011 [7]  1.52 (89 of 5,869)  Anomalous origin of RCA from LSV  64MDCT  Japan
Sivri et al. 2012 [8]  0.74 (95 of 12,814)  LCX arising from RSV or RCA  CAG  Turkey
Sohrabi et al. 2012 [9]  1.30 (79 of 6,065)  Absent LMCA with separate origin of LAD and LCX  CAG  Iran
Xu et al. 2012 [10]  1.02 (124 of 12,415)  Anomalous origin of RCA from LSV  Dual-source CTCA  China
Yukel et al. 2013 [11]  0.29 (48 of 16,573)  Anomalous origin of LCX from RCA/RSV  CAG  Turkey
2000 [4] reported a prevalence of 0.95% (39 of 4,100) for the anomalous origin of RCA from LSV/NAS via CAG in India.
Yildiz et al. 2010 [5] indicated a prevalence of 0.90% (112 of 12,457) for absent LMCA with separate origin of LAD and LCX,
using CAG in Turkey. Erol et al. 2011 [6] had a prevalence of 1.96% (53 of 2,096) for absent LMCA with separate origin of
LAD and LCX/Origin of RCA from LSV, utilizing 64MDCT in Turkey. Fujimoto et al. 2011 [7] reported a prevalence of 1.52%
(89 of 5,869) for anomalous origin of RCA from LSV, using 64MDCT in Japan. Sivri et al. 2012 [8] documented a prevalence
of 0.74% (95 of 12,814) for LCX arising from RSV or RCA via CAG in Turkey. Sohrabi et al. 2012 [9] reported a prevalence of
1.30% (79 of 6,065) for absent LMCA with separate origin of LAD and LCX, using CAG in Iran. Xu et al. 2012 [10] found a
prevalence of 1.02% (124 of 12,415) for anomalous origin of RCA from LSV, utilizing dual-source CTCA in China. Finally,
Yukel et al. 2013 [11] reported a prevalence of 0.29% (48 of 16,573) for anomalous origin of LCX from RCA/RSV, employing
CAG in Turkey.
